top_signals.title: Moody’s Cuts Mexico Credit Rating to One Notch Above Junk
top_signals.summary: Moody’s Ratings cut Mexico’s credit score to the lowest tier of investment grade, citing the country’s weakening fiscal position and stoking concern Latin America’s second-largest economy is heading toward junk status.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
